perturbation.original_text: Euler Hermes shall keep the Confidential Information confidential and may disclose the Confidential Information only to its Affiliates , employees, contractors , or consultants for the Purpose described above and no other purpose. Euler Hermes shall be liable for any breach of confidentiality by any Affiliate, employee, contractor , or consultant.
perturbation.changed_text: Euler Hermes shall endeavor to keep the Confidential Information confidential and may disclose the Confidential Information to its Affiliates , employees, contractors , or consultants for the Purpose described above and no other purpose. Euler Hermes will try to ensure its Affiliates, employees, contractors, or consultants respect the confidentiality of the information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Euler Hermes 'shall keep' the information confidential and 'shall be liable' for breaches. The modified text weakens this by saying Euler Hermes 'shall endeavor to keep' the information confidential and will 'try to ensure' confidentiality by its affiliates. This makes the obligation less strict and introduces ambiguity, as 'endeavor' and 'try' are not well-defined legal standards.
perturbation.law_explanation: MUTSA requires reasonable efforts to maintain secrecy to qualify for trade secret protection. Replacing 'shall keep' with 'shall endeavor' weakens the standard of care expected and might not meet the 'reasonable efforts' standard required under MUTSA, potentially jeopardizing trade secret protection if confidentiality is breached. The original language ensures a stronger legal obligation.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 11-1203 (2022) - Right to Damages :: 2022 Maryland Code :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Maryland Code 2023 Maryland Code 2022 Maryland Code (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Maryland Statutes Commercial Law Title 11 - Trade Regulation Subtitle 12 - Maryland Uniform Trade Secrets Act Section 11-1203 - Right to Damages Universal Citation: MD Commercial Law Code § 11-1203 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next (a) Except to the extent that a material and prejudicial change of position prior to acquiring knowledge or reason to know of misappropriation renders a monetary recovery inequitable, a complainant is entitled to recover damages for misappropriation. (b) Damages under this subtitle may include: (1) The actual loss caused by misappropriation; and (2) The unjust enrichment caused by misappropriation that is not taken into account in computing actual loss. (c) In lieu of damages measured by any other methods, the damages caused by misappropriation may be measured by imposition of liability for a reasonable royalty for a misappropriator’s unauthorized disclosure or use of a trade secret. (d) If willful and malicious misappropriation exists, the court may award exemplary damages in an amount not exceeding twice any award made under subsection (a) of this section. Previous Next Disclaimer: These codes may not be the most recent version. Maryland may have more current or accurate information. We make no warranties or guarantees about the accuracy, completeness, or adequacy of the information contained on this site or the information linked to on the state site. perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall keep' and 'shall be liable' to 'shall endeavor to keep' and 'try to ensure' significantly weakens the legal obligation to maintain confidentiality. The Law Explanation suggests this weakening may not meet the 'reasonable efforts' standard required for trade secret protection under MUTSA, potentially jeopardizing that protection if confidentiality is breached. Scraped Law Snippet 2, Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 11-1203, outlines the 'Right to Damages' for misappropriation, implying a standard of care. By weakening the language, the modified text creates ambiguity about the standard of care required, potentially undermining the ability to claim damages in case of a breach. The change introduces ambiguity in the legal obligation, thus creating a contradiction under the Ambiguities - Ambiguous Legal Obligation definition.

perturbation.original_text: Euler Hermes shall protect the Confidential Information from disclos ure to any firm or individual other than as authorized in paragraph 1, above, by using the same degree of care, but no less than reasonable care, that it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature . Euler Hermes will instruct its Affiliates, employees, contractors, or consultants that may have access to the Confidential Information to keep it confidential in compliance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Euler Hermes will consider protecting the Confidential Information from disclosure to any firm or individual other than as authorized in paragraph 1, above, using care they deem appropriate. Euler Hermes may inform its Affiliates, employees, contractors, or consultants that may have access to the Confidential Information of the need to keep it confidential.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates Euler Hermes 'shall protect' and use 'no less than reasonable care'. The modification replaces this with 'will consider protecting' and 'care they deem appropriate', which introduces subjective judgment. Similarly, 'will instruct' is weakened to 'may inform,' making the action discretionary. The original text ensures a higher degree of obligation to protect the confidential information.
perturbation.law_explanation: By weakening the obligation to protect confidential information and making the protective measures discretionary, the modified text potentially fails to meet the reasonable efforts standard for maintaining trade secret protection under MUTSA. 'Care they deem appropriate' could be interpreted as a lower standard than 'reasonable care,' and the discretionary nature of informing affiliates further undermines the efforts.

perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation to protect confidential information, creating a potential contradiction with trade secret law. The original text used "shall protect" and "no less than reasonable care," creating a clear obligation. The changed text uses "will consider protecting" and "care they deem appropriate," and weakens "will instruct" to "may inform." This introduces discretion and subjectivity, potentially failing to meet the standard of reasonable efforts to maintain trade secret protection. Maryland Commercial Law Code § 11-1203 addresses damages for misappropriation, implying a need for reasonable protection measures. The change from mandatory protection to discretionary consideration undermines this requirement.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the Company’s written request, Euler Hermes shall promptly return to the Company or destroy the Confidential Information in its possession but may retain copies of any and all notes, analyses , references, or other material prepared by Euler Hermes that inc orporates any of the Confidential Information , which shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement , nothwithstanding Paragraph 10 of this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Company’s written request, Euler Hermes will endeavor to return to the Company or destroy the Confidential Information in its possession and may retain copies of any and all notes, analyses , references, or other material prepared by Euler Hermes that incorporates any of the Confidential Information , which should remain subject to confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses the term 'shall promptly return' creating a definitive obligation. The modified text uses the term 'will endeavor to return' creating a weaker, less binding obligation. Additionally, the phrase 'which shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement' is changed to 'which should remain subject to confidentiality' which is a suggestion, not a requirement. These changes introduces ambiguity and weakens the protection of the confidential information.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modifications weaken the obligation to return or destroy confidential information promptly and weaken the obligation of confidentiality regarding retained copies. This potentially fails to maintain the reasonable efforts to protect the confidentiality of the information as a trade secret under MUTSA.

perturbation.justification: The original contract language, 'shall promptly return,' creates a definitive obligation, while the changed text, 'will endeavor to return,' introduces ambiguity and weakens this obligation. Additionally, the phrase 'which shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement' is changed to 'which should remain subject to confidentiality,' which transforms a requirement into a suggestion. This undermines the reasonable efforts to protect confidential information, as suggested by the Law Explanation. The Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 11-1203 addresses damages for misappropriation, implying a need for robust confidentiality protections, which are weakened by the changes. The alteration from 'shall' to 'will endeavor' and from 'shall remain' to 'should remain' reduces the enforceability of the confidentiality provisions.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be exclusively governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the state of Maryland, and the parties agree to submit to the jurisdiction of the courts of that state for the resolution of any claim or matter arising under this Agreement
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, and interpreted in a manner consistent with, general principles of law, and the parties will consider the jurisdiction of the courts of Maryland for the resolution of any claim or matter arising under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text unequivocally states that the agreement 'shall be exclusively governed by' Maryland law and 'agree to submit to the jurisdiction' of Maryland courts. The modified version weakens this by saying the agreement 'shall be governed by...general principles of law' and the parties 'will consider the jurisdiction' of Maryland courts. This introduces ambiguity by potentially allowing for interpretations under laws other than Maryland's and makes the agreement to jurisdiction discretionary.
perturbation.law_explanation: Maryland generally honors choice-of-law provisions in contracts. By changing the governing law to 'general principles of law' and making jurisdiction discretionary, the modified text creates ambiguity that could conflict with Maryland's established approach to enforcing contractual choice-of-law clauses. Maryland courts give significant weight to the parties' chosen forum, and making it discretionary undermines this.
perturbation.justification: The original text firmly establishes Maryland law as the exclusive governing law and mandates submission to Maryland courts. The changed text introduces ambiguity by switching to 'general principles of law' and making the jurisdictional consideration discretionary. Although there are no specific scraped law snippets provided, the perturbation and law explanations highlight that Maryland law typically honors choice-of-law provisions. By weakening the governing law and jurisdiction clauses, the modified contract potentially conflicts with Maryland's approach, and undermines the legal obligation, which is the definition of Ambiguities Legal Contradiction.
